structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Plant Enable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Enable the chiller plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes, the number of Chiller Plant Requests exceeds the ignored requests threshold, outdoor air temperature is above the lockout temperature, and the enable schedule is active.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes, Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (default 0, adjustable), OAT > CH-LOT, and chiller plant enable schedule is active.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable the plant in the lowest stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT>CH-LOT, and c. The chiller plant enable schedule is active.

evidence_quote: Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT>CH-LOT, and c. The chiller plant enable schedule is active.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Plant Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Disable the chiller plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and either the number of Chiller Plant Requests drops to or below the ignored requests threshold for 3 minutes, outdoor air temperature drops below the lockout temperature minus 1°F, or the enable schedule becomes inactive.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Plant has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and (Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F, or chiller plant enable schedule is inactive).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Disable the plant.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.

evidence_quote: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Stage Up – Efficiency Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage up when the current stage OPLR exceeds SPLR UP for 15 minutes and the OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Initiate a stage-up event.
configurable_values: SPLR UP (stage up part load ratio, varies by chiller type and lift)
OPLR decreasing rate threshold (2.5% per minute)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Efficiency Condition : Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; or

evidence_quote: Efficiency Condition : Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; or

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Stage Down – Efficiency Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage down when the next available lower stage OPLR is below SPLR DN for 15 minutes and the next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes, and the failsafe stage up condition is not true.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes, and the failsafe stage up condition is not true.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Initiate a stage-down event.
configurable_values: SPLR DN (stage down part load ratio, varies by chiller type and lift)
OPLR increasing rate threshold (2.5% per minute)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.

evidence_quote: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Reset – Differential Pressure Controlled Loops
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Reset CHWST setpoint and pump DP setpoint based on the CHW Plant Reset variable. From 0% to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax. From 50% to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin. The CHW Plant Reset variable is controlled by a Trim & Respond loop using cooling CHWST reset requests.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Plant is enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax over 0-50% loop output; reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin over 50-100% loop output. The CHW Plant Reset variable is adjusted by Trim & Respond logic.
configurable_values: Trim & Respond parameters (SP0, SPmin, SPmax, Td, T, I, SPtrim, SPres, SPres-max)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

evidence_quote: a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Fan Control – CWRT Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control cooling tower fan speed to maintain condenser water return temperature (CWRT) at a setpoint that is reset based on plant part load ratio and chiller lift requirements. Fan speed is limited by head pressure control and part load ratio.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any condenser water pump is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Maintain CWRT at setpoint using a direct acting PID loop. The setpoint is calculated as CWRTsp = CHWSTsp + LIFTtarget, where LIFTtarget is a function of PLRplant. Tower speed command is the lowest of CWRTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd from each chiller, and PlrTowerMaxSpd.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When any condenser water pump is proven on, CWRT shall be maintained at setpoint by a direct acting PID loop. The loop output shall be mapped to the variable CWRTTowerSpd. Map CWRTTowerSpd from minimum tower speed at 0% loop output to 100% speed at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: When any condenser water pump is proven on, CWRT shall be maintained at setpoint by a direct acting PID loop. The loop output shall be mapped to the variable CWRTTowerSpd. Map CWRTTowerSpd from minimum tower speed at 0% loop output to 100% speed at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Waterside Economizer Enable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Enable the waterside economizer if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and the chilled water return temperature upstream of the heat exchanger is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature plus 2°F.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: WSE has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX > PHXLWT + 2°F.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable the waterside economizer.
configurable_values: PHXLWT calculation parameters (DA HX, DT WB, DA CT, m)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.

evidence_quote: Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Waterside Economizer Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Disable the waterside economizer when it has run for at least 20 minutes and the chilled water temperature downstream of the heat exchanger is greater than the chilled water return temperature upstream of the heat exchanger less 1°F for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: WSE has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX > CHWRT upstream of HX less 1°F for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Disable the waterside economizer.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes (i.e., if the HX is not reducing the CHW temp by at least 1°F).

evidence_quote: Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes (i.e., if the HX is not reducing the CHW temp by at least 1°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Head Pressure Control – Fixed Speed CW Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For fixed speed condenser water pumps, map the head pressure control loop output to reset maximum cooling tower speed from 100% to minimum speed over 0-50% output, and reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos over 50-100% output.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Head pressure control loop is enabled for an operating chiller.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: From 0-50% loop output, reset HpTowerMaxSpd from 100% to minimum speed. From 50-100% loop output, reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.
configurable_values: MinCWVlvPos (minimum head pressure control valve position)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.

evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Head Pressure Control – Variable Speed CW Pumps (No WSE)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For variable speed condenser water pumps without a waterside economizer, map the head pressure control loop output to reset maximum cooling tower speed from 100% to minimum speed over 0-50% output, and reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed over 50-100% output.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Head pressure control loop is enabled for an operating chiller.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: From 0-50% loop output, reset HpTowerMaxSpd from 100% to minimum speed. From 50-100% loop output, reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed.
configurable_values: Cw-DesPumpSpdStage (design CW pump speed for stage)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed.

evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Head Pressure Control – With Waterside Economizer (WSE Disabled)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the waterside economizer is disabled, map the head pressure control loop output to reset maximum cooling tower speed from 100% to minimum speed over 0-50% output, and reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed over 50-100% output. Head pressure control valves are 100% open.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Head pressure control loop is enabled and WSE is disabled.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: From 0-50% loop output, reset HpTowerMaxSpd from 100% to minimum speed. From 50-100% loop output, reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed. Each enabled chiller's head pressure control valve shall be 100% open.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed. c. Note: Each enabled chiller’s head pressure control valve shall be 100% open irrespective of loop output.

evidence_quote: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset condenser water pump speed from Cw-DesPumpSpdStage to MinCWPspeed. c. Note: Each enabled chiller’s head pressure control valve shall be 100% open irrespective of loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Head Pressure Control – With Waterside Economizer (WSE Enabled)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the waterside economizer is enabled, set maximum cooling tower speed to 100%, set condenser water pump speed to design speed for the stage, and map the head pressure control loop output to reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Head pressure control loop is enabled and WSE is enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: HpTowerMaxSpeed = 100%. Condenser water pump speed = design speed for the stage. Each enabled chiller's head pressure control loop output resets head pressure control valve position from 100% open at 0% loop output to MinCWVlvPos at 100% loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpeed, shall be 100% irrespective of loop output. b. Condenser water pump speed shall be equal to the design speed for the stage irrespective of loop output. c. Each enabled chiller’s head pressure control loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open at 0% loop output to MinCWVlvPos at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: a. Maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpeed, shall be 100% irrespective of loop output. b. Condenser water pump speed shall be equal to the design speed for the stage irrespective of loop output. c. Each enabled chiller’s head pressure control loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open at 0% loop output to MinCWVlvPos at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Primary CHW Pump Staging – Headered Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage primary chilled water pumps based on the ratio of current flow to design flow. Start the next lag pump when CHWFR > (Number of Operating Pumps / N) - 0.03 for 10 minutes. Shut off the last lag pump when CHWFR ≤ (Number of Operating Pumps - 1) / N - 0.03 for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: CHWFR > (Number of Operating Pumps / N) - 0.03 for 10 minutes (stage up) or CHWFR ≤ (Number of Operating Pumps - 1) / N - 0.03 for 10 minutes (stage down).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Start the next lag pump (stage up) or shut off the last lag pump (stage down).
configurable_values: N (number of pumps at design conditions)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Start the next lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: CHWFR > Number of Operating Pumps / N - .03 b. Shut off the last lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: CHWFR ≤ Number of Operating Pumps - 1 / N - .03

evidence_quote: a. Start the next lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: CHWFR > Number of Operating Pumps / N - .03 b. Shut off the last lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: CHWFR ≤ Number of Operating Pumps - 1 / N - .03

structured_payload_candidate.title: Primary CHW Pump Speed Control – DP Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When any primary CHW pump is proven on, control pump speed with a reverse acting PID loop to maintain differential pressure at setpoint CHW-DPsp. All pumps receive the same speed signal.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any primary CHW pump is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Control pump speed to maintain differential pressure at CHW-DPsp using a reverse acting PID loop. All pumps receive the same speed signal.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When any pump is proven on, pump speed will be controlled by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining the differential pressure signal at a setpoint CHW-DPsp determined by the reset scheme described herein. All pumps receive the same speed signal.

evidence_quote: When any pump is proven on, pump speed will be controlled by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining the differential pressure signal at a setpoint CHW-DPsp determined by the reset scheme described herein. All pumps receive the same speed signal.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Secondary CHW Pump Staging – Headered Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage secondary chilled water pumps based on the ratio of current flow to design flow. Start the next lag pump when SCHWFR > (Number of Operating Pumps / N) - 0.03 for 10 minutes. Shut off the last lag pump when SCHWFR < (Number of Operating Pumps - 1) / N - 0.03 for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SCHWFR > (Number of Operating Pumps / N) - 0.03 for 10 minutes (stage up) or SCHWFR < (Number of Operating Pumps - 1) / N - 0.03 for 10 minutes (stage down).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Start the next lag pump (stage up) or shut off the last lag pump (stage down).
configurable_values: N (number of pumps at design conditions)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Start the next lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: SCHWFR > Number of Operating Pumps / N - .03 2. Shut off the last lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: SCHWFR < Number of Operating Pumps - 1 / N - .03

evidence_quote: 1. Start the next lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: SCHWFR > Number of Operating Pumps / N - .03 2. Shut off the last lag pump whenever the following is true for 10 minutes: SCHWFR < Number of Operating Pumps - 1 / N - .03

structured_payload_candidate.title: Secondary CHW Pump Speed Control – DP Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When any secondary CHW pump is proven on, control pump speed with a reverse acting PID loop to maintain differential pressure at setpoint CHW-DPsp. All pumps receive the same speed signal.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any secondary CHW pump is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Control pump speed to maintain differential pressure at CHW-DPsp using a reverse acting PID loop. All pumps receive the same speed signal.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. When any secondary CHW pump is proven on, pump speed will be controlled by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining the differential pressure signal at a setpoint CHW-DPsp determined by the reset scheme described herein. All pumps receive the same speed signal.

evidence_quote: b. When any secondary CHW pump is proven on, pump speed will be controlled by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining the differential pressure signal at a setpoint CHW-DPsp determined by the reset scheme described herein. All pumps receive the same speed signal.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Minimum Flow Bypass Valve Control – Parallel Chillers
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Chilled Water Minimum Flow Bypass Valve
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Modulate the bypass valve to maintain minimum flow through all operating chillers. The minimum flow setpoint is calculated as the highest MinFlowRatio (CHW-MinFlowX / CHW-DesFlowX) among operating chillers multiplied by the sum of their CHW-DesFlowX.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any CHW pump is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate bypass valve to maintain flow at setpoint = Max(MinFlowRatio) * Sum(CHW-DesFlowX for operating chillers).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Bypass valve shall modulate to maintain minimum flow as measured by the chilled water flow meter at a setpoint that provides minimum flow through all operating chillers, determined as follows: a. For the chillers operating in the stage, identify the chiller with the highest ratio, MinFlowRatio, of CHW-MinFlowX to CHW-DesFlowX. b. Calculate the minimum flow setpoint as MinFlowRatio multiplied by the sum of CHW-DesFlowX for the operating chillers.

evidence_quote: Bypass valve shall modulate to maintain minimum flow as measured by the chilled water flow meter at a setpoint that provides minimum flow through all operating chillers, determined as follows: a. For the chillers operating in the stage, identify the chiller with the highest ratio, MinFlowRatio, of CHW-MinFlowX to CHW-DesFlowX. b. Calculate the minimum flow setpoint as MinFlowRatio multiplied by the sum of CHW-DesFlowX for the operating chillers.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Staging
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage cooling tower cells based on plant stage. Lead cells are enabled when the lead CW pump is enabled and disabled when all CW pumps are off. Tower stage changes are initiated concurrently with condenser water pump changes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Lead CW pump is enabled (stage up) or all CW pumps are off (stage down).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable lead tower cell(s) when lead CW pump is enabled. Disable lead tower cell(s) when all CW pumps are off. Tower stage changes occur concurrently with CW pump stage/speed changes.
configurable_values: Plant stage to tower cell mapping table
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. Lead cell(s) shall be enabled when the lead CW pump is enabled. Lead cell(s) shall be disabled when all CW pumps are proven off. d. Tower stage changes shall be initiated concurrently with condenser water pump stage and/or condenser water pump speed changes per plant staging logic in Section 5.20.4.

evidence_quote: c. Lead cell(s) shall be enabled when the lead CW pump is enabled. Lead cell(s) shall be disabled when all CW pumps are proven off. d. Tower stage changes shall be initiated concurrently with condenser water pump stage and/or condenser water pump speed changes per plant staging logic in Section 5.20.4.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Tower Fan Enable/Disable – CWRT Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Disable tower fans if any enabled chiller's HpTowerMaxSpd has been at minimum for 5 minutes, or if fans have been at minimum speed for 5 minutes and CWRT drops below setpoint minus 1°F. Enable fans if they have been off for at least 1 minute, CWRT rises above setpoint by 1°F, and all enabled chillers' HpTowerMaxSpd are above minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Disable: (Any enabled chiller's HpTowerMaxSpd at minimum for 5 minutes) OR (fans at minimum for 5 minutes AND CWRT < setpoint - 1°F). Enable: Fans off for ≥1 minute AND CWRT > setpoint + 1°F AND all enabled chillers' HpTowerMaxSpd > minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Disable or enable tower fans based on conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 11. Disable the tower fans if either i. Any enabled chiller’s HpTowerMaxSpd has equaled tower minimum speed for 5 minutes, or ii. Tower fans have been at minimum speed for 5 minutes and CWRT drops below setpoint minus 1°F. 12. Enable the tower fans if i. They have been off for at least 1 minute, and ii. CWRT rises above setpoint by 1°F, and iv. All enabled chillers’ HpTowerMaxSpd are greater than tower minimum speed.

evidence_quote: 11. Disable the tower fans if either i. Any enabled chiller’s HpTowerMaxSpd has equaled tower minimum speed for 5 minutes, or ii. Tower fans have been at minimum speed for 5 minutes and CWRT drops below setpoint minus 1°F. 12. Enable the tower fans if i. They have been off for at least 1 minute, and ii. CWRT rises above setpoint by 1°F, and iv. All enabled chillers’ HpTowerMaxSpd are greater than tower minimum speed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Tower Make-up Water Valve Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cycle the make-up water valve based on tower water fill level sensor. Open the valve when water level falls below T-level-min-fill. Close the valve when water level rises above T-level-max-fill.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Water level < T-level-min-fill (open) or water level > T-level-max-fill (close).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Open make-up water valve when water level is below T-level-min-fill. Close valve when water level is above T-level-max-fill.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Make-up water valve shall cycle based on tower water fill level sensor. The valve shall open when water level falls below T-level-min-fill. It shall close when the water level goes above T-level-max-fill.

evidence_quote: Make-up water valve shall cycle based on tower water fill level sensor. The valve shall open when water level falls below T-level-min-fill. It shall close when the water level goes above T-level-max-fill.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Tower Basin Heater Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Enable the basin heater when basin temperature drops below 38°F. Disable the basin heater when basin temperature rises above 40°F.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Basin temperature < 38°F (enable) or basin temperature > 40°F (disable).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable basin heater when temperature < 38°F. Disable when temperature > 40°F.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Enable basin heater whenever basin temperature drops below 38°F. b. Disable basin heater whenever basin temperature rises above 40°F.

evidence_quote: a. Enable basin heater whenever basin temperature drops below 38°F. b. Disable basin heater whenever basin temperature rises above 40°F.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Piping Heat Trace Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Enable piping heat trace when outdoor air temperature drops below 34°F. Disable heat trace when outdoor air temperature rises above 40°F.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: OAT < 34°F (enable) or OAT > 40°F (disable).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable heat trace when OAT < 34°F. Disable when OAT > 40°F.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. Enable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature drops below 34°F. b. Disable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature rises above 40°F.

evidence_quote: a. Enable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature drops below 34°F. b. Disable heat trace whenever outdoor air temperature rises above 40°F.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#1: DP too high with pumps off
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if differential pressure is too high when chilled water pumps are off, indicating a possible DP sensor error.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: DP AVG > Ɛ DSP and Status CHWP = Off
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#1 Equation DP AVG > Ɛ DSP and Status CHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Differential pressure is too high with the chilled water pumps off Possible Diagnosis DP sensor error

evidence_quote: FC#1 Equation DP AVG > Ɛ DSP and Status CHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Differential pressure is too high with the chilled water pumps off Possible Diagnosis DP sensor error

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#4: DP too low with pumps at full speed
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if chilled water loop differential pressure is too low with pumps at full speed, indicating possible VFD, mechanical, or sizing issues.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: DP AVG < DPSP – ƐDP and Speed CHWP ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#4 Equation DP AVG < DPSP – ƐDP and Speed CHWP ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water loop differential pressure is too low with chilled water pump(s) at full speed.

evidence_quote: FC#4 Equation DP AVG < DPSP – ƐDP and Speed CHWP ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water loop differential pressure is too low with chilled water pump(s) at full speed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#6: CHWST too high
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if chilled water supply temperature is too high, indicating possible mechanical problems with chillers or excessive primary flow.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with chillers Primary flow is higher than the design evaporator flow of the operating chillers

evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with chillers Primary flow is higher than the design evaporator flow of the operating chillers

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#8: Condenser approach too high
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if condenser approach temperature is too high, indicating possible condenser fouling, blocked tubes, or low water temperature/flow.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#8 Equation Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Condenser approach is too high Possible Diagnosis Possible condenser fouling or blocked condenser tubes Low condenser water temperature Low condenser water flow

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#9: Evaporator approach too high
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if evaporator approach temperature is too high, indicating possible evaporator fouling, blocked tubes, or low refrigerant charge.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#14: CWST too high with towers at full speed

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#16: Heat exchanger approach high
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if waterside economizer heat exchanger approach is high, indicating possible fouling or blocked tubes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#18: Too many OS changes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if the number of changes in Operating State exceeds the maximum, indicating unstable control or mechanical problems.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#18 Equation ΔOS > ΔOS MAX Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description Too many changes in Operating State Possible Diagnosis Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#19: Too many chiller starts
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if a chiller has started too many times in the last 60 minutes, indicating possible cycling due to low load or oversized chiller.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#19 Equation ΔStartsCH-x > ΔCHStart MAX Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Too many chiller starts Possible Diagnosis Chiller is cycling due to load loads. Chiller is oversized and/or has insufficient turndown capability. Chiller stage-up threshold may be set too low.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant AFDD – FC#20: Too many stage changes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Detects if the number of plant stage changes exceeds the maximum, indicating staging thresholds or delays need adjustment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish CHW-DPmax
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fully open downstream control valves, close upstream valves, start pumps, and adjust speed until design flow is achieved through all open coils. Record the BAS differential pressure sensor reading as CHW-DPmax.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Fully open all control valves serving coils that are located downstream of the differential pressure sensor. Close the minimum flow bypass valve (if applicable). Fully close all control valves serving coils that are located upstream of the differential pressure sensor. Start pump(s). Manually adjust speed slowly until design flow (or design pressure drop, for coils without calibrated balance valves) is just achieved through all open coils without modulating any balance valves. One coil should be just at design flow, while others should be at or above design flow. Once flow condition in previous step is achieved, note the BAS differential pressure sensor and handheld digital pressure sensor readings to verify accuracy of BAS reading; report BAS reading to controls contractor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Fully open all control valves serving coils that are located downstream of the differential pressure sensor. 2. Close the minimum flow bypass valve (if applicable). 3. Fully close all control valves serving coils that are located upstream of the differential pressure sensor. 4. Start pump(s). Manually adjust speed slowly until design flow (or design pressure drop, for coils without calibrated balance valves) is just achieved through all open coils without modulating any balance valves. One coil should be just at design flow, while others should be at or above design flow. 5. Once flow condition in previous step is achieved, note the BAS differential pressure sensor and handheld digital pressure sensor readings to verify accuracy of BAS reading; report BAS reading to controls contractor.

evidence_quote: 1. Fully open all control valves serving coils that are located downstream of the differential pressure sensor. 2. Close the minimum flow bypass valve (if applicable). 3. Fully close all control valves serving coils that are located upstream of the differential pressure sensor. 4. Start pump(s). Manually adjust speed slowly until design flow (or design pressure drop, for coils without calibrated balance valves) is just achieved through all open coils without modulating any balance valves. One coil should be just at design flow, while others should be at or above design flow. 5. Once flow condition in previous step is achieved, note the BAS differential pressure sensor and handheld digital pressure sensor readings to verify accuracy of BAS reading; report BAS reading to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish Cw-DesPumpSpdStage
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For each chiller stage, configure the plant to that stage, reduce CW pump speed until condenser water flow through any chiller drops to 100% of design, and record the speed setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Configure the plant to the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the CW isolation valve of the chiller(s) in the stage and enable the CW pump(s) to operate in the stage and the corresponding number of cooling towers for the stage. 2. Reduce CW pump speed in 2% increments until condenser water flow through any chiller with an open CW isolation valve drops to 100% of design as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor. 4. Repeat the preceding steps for each chiller stage.

Open the CW isolation valve of the chiller (s) in the stage and enable the CW pump(s) to operate in the stage and the corresponding number of cooling towers for the stage. 2. Reduce CW pump speed in 2% increments until condenser water flow through any chiller with an open CW isolation valve drops to 100% of design as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor. 4. Repeat the preceding steps for each chiller stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish MinCWVlvPos (Constant Speed CW Pumps)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For constant speed CW pumps, configure the plant for the lowest stage, throttle the head pressure control valve until flow through the chiller's condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum flow switch setpoint, and record the valve position.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Configure the plant for the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the head pressure control valve of the test chiller and enable a CW pump and the corresponding number of cooling towers for that stage. 2. Throttle the head pressure control valve of the test chiller in 2% increments until the flow rate through the chiller’s condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum condenser water flow switch setpoint as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the valve position setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish HxPumpDesSpd
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For waterside economizer HX pumps, decrease pump speed from 100% until differential pressure across the HX is within 5% of design, and record the speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Starting from 100% speed, decrease HX pump speed in 2% increments until differential pressure measured across the waterside economizer heat exchanger is within 5% of design. 2. Note the design speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Starting from 100% speed, decrease HX pump speed in 2% increments until differential pressure measured across the waterside economizer heat exchanger is within 5% of design. 2. Note the design speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish Ch-MaxPriPumpSpdStage
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For primary CHW pumps intended to operate at fixed speeds, decrease pump speed from 100% until differential pressure across the chiller evaporator is within 5% of design, and record the speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Starting from 100% speed, decrease primary chilled water pump speed in 2% increments until differential pressure measured across the chiller evaporator is within 5% of design. 2. Note the design speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Starting from 100% speed, decrease primary chilled water pump speed in 2% increments until differential pressure measured across the chiller evaporator is within 5% of design. 2. Note the design speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish Ch-MinPriPumpSpdStage
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For variable primary-variable secondary systems, open all chiller isolation valves, start all non-redundant primary pumps at 100% speed, decrease speed until flow through any chiller decreases to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX, and record the speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Open isolation valves of all chillers. 2. Start all non-redundant primary pumps at 100% speed. 3. Decrease primary pump speed in 2% increments starting from 100% until flowrate through any chiller, as measured by either evaporator barrel differential pressure or a flow meter, decreases to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX. 4. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Open isolation valves of all chillers. 2. Start all non-redundant primary pumps at 100% speed. 3. Decrease primary pump speed in 2% increments starting from 100% until flowrate through any chiller, as measured by either evaporator barrel differential pressure or a flow meter, decreases to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX. 4. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

evidence_quote: 1. Open isolation valves of all chillers. 2. Start all non-redundant primary pumps at 100% speed. 3. Decrease primary pump speed in 2% increments starting from 100% until flowrate through any chiller, as measured by either evaporator barrel differential pressure or a flow meter, decreases to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX. 4. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Establish Minimum Speeds for VFD-driven Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For VFD-driven pumps and tower fans, manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%), observe rotation, and gradually increase until visibly rotating. That speed becomes the minimum speed setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: 1. Start the fan or pump. 2. Manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%) unless otherwise indicated in control sequences. For equipment with gear boxes, use whatever minimum speed is recommended by the manufacturer. 3. Observe fan/pump in field to ensure it is visibly rotating. If not, gradually increase speed until it is. 4. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Start the fan or pump. 2. Manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%) unless otherwise indicated in control sequences. For equipment with gear boxes, use whatever minimum speed is recommended by manufacturer. 3. Observe fan/pump in field to ensure it is visibly rotating. If not, gradually increase speed until it is. 4. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

If not, gradually increase speed until it is. 4. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant Reset Logic – DP First, Then Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The recommended logic first resets differential pressure setpoint to maximum before resetting chilled water supply temperature setpoint down towards design. This order prioritizes chiller energy savings over pump energy savings.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The recommended logic first resets differential pressure setpoint to maximum before resetting chilled water supply temperature setpoint down towards design. Parametric plant analysis performed in a variety of climate zones during the development of ASHRAE’s “Fundamentals of Design and Control of Central Chilled-Water Plants” Self-Directed Learning Course showed that the pump energy penalty incurred with this approach is more than offset by chiller energy savings resulting from keeping the chilled water supply temperature setpoint as high as possible.

evidence_quote: The recommended logic first resets differential pressure setpoint to maximum before resetting chilled water supply temperature setpoint down towards design. Parametric plant analysis performed in a variety of climate zones during the development of ASHRAE’s “Fundamentals of Design and Control of Central Chilled-Water Plants” Self-Directed Learning Course showed that the pump energy penalty incurred with this approach is more than offset by chiller energy savings resulting from keeping the chilled water supply temperature setpoint as high as possible.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant Reset – Starting Point
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The reset starts at CHWSTmin because starting at a high temperature often causes the chiller to bring down CHWST too quickly and pass the setpoint, leading to cycling. Additionally, starting at a high temperature may delay satisfying the load.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The reset starts at CHWSTmin because starting at a high temperature often causes the chiller to bring down CHWST too quickly and pass the CHWST setpoint, leading the chiller to cycle off. Additionally, if the loop reset starts at a CHWST that cannot satisfy the load at startup (e.g., CHWST setpoint = 60°F, but an AHU requires 55°F supply air), there is a resultant delay in satisfying the load as the reset loop winds up before CHWST setpoint resets down.

evidence_quote: The reset starts at CHWSTmin because starting at a high temperature often causes the chiller to bring down CHWST too quickly and pass the CHWST setpoint, leading the chiller to cycle off. Additionally, if the loop reset starts at a CHWST that cannot satisfy the load at startup (e.g., CHWST setpoint = 60°F, but an AHU requires 55°F supply air), there is a resultant delay in satisfying the load as the reset loop winds up before CHWST setpoint resets down.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Staging Rules – Chiller Type Precedence
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Staging rules for constant speed centrifugal chillers supersede those for positive displacement chillers, which supersede those for variable speed centrifugal chillers. This hierarchy reflects typical plant design where positive displacement machines handle low loads, variable speed centrifugals are next, and constant speed centrifugals are last.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The above section effectively means the rules for staging constant speed centrifugal chillers supersede the rules for staging positive displacement chillers, and the rules for staging positive displacement chillers supersede the rules for staging variable speed centrifugal chillers. These rules assume the following staging hierarchy applies globally across chiller plants based on current industry best practice: (1) If the plant has any positive displacement machines, those are staged on first since they are generally sized to handle low load conditions. (2) Variable speed centrifugal machines are staged on next. (3) Constant speed centrifugal machines are staged on last.

evidence_quote: The above section effectively means the rules for staging constant speed centrifugal chillers supersede the rules for staging positive displacement chillers, and the rules for staging positive displacement chillers supersede the rules for staging variable speed centrifugal chillers. These rules assume the following staging hierarchy applies globally across chiller plants based on current industry best practice: (1) If the plant has any positive displacement machines, those are staged on first since they are generally sized to handle low load conditions. (2) Variable speed centrifugal machines are staged on next. (3) Constant speed centrifugal machines are staged on last.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Variable Speed Centrifugal Chiller Staging Point Reset with Lift
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For variable speed centrifugal chillers, the staging point (SPLR UP) is reset based on lift to optimize efficiency. Higher lift increases the staging point to minimize throttling losses from surge control devices.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Centrifugal chiller efficiency varies significantly with lift. As lift increases for a given load, centrifugal compressors must run faster to avoid surge. Capacity trimming under such conditions is accomplished using inlet guide vanes or variable geometry diffusers, which reduces chiller efficiency. The above equation resets the centrifugal staging point up when lift is high to minimize throttling of surge control devices and keep chillers operating near to their optimal efficiency.

evidence_quote: Centrifugal chiller efficiency varies significantly with lift. As lift increases for a given load, centrifugal compressors must run faster to avoid surge. Capacity trimming under such conditions is accomplished using inlet guide vanes or variable geometry diffusers, which reduces chiller efficiency. The above equation resets the centrifugal staging point up when lift is high to minimize throttling of surge control devices and keep chillers operating near to their optimal efficiency.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Primary-Secondary Staging – Avoid Secondary Recirculation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Primary-secondary staging logic stages up when secondary CHWST exceeds primary CHWST by 2°F and primary pumps are at maximum speed, to avoid the positive feedback loop of secondary recirculation.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Primary-secondary staging needs to ensure that secondary flow does not exceed primary flow. When secondary flow exceeds primary flow, the secondary CHWST degrades (elevates), in turn causing lower secondary CHWRT. This in turn decreases the load on the chillers while causing the secondary flowrate to only increase further. Staging logic avoids this positive feedback scenario by staging up when the presence of secondary recirculation has been confirmed by secondary CHWST exceeding primary by 2°F.

evidence_quote: Primary-secondary staging needs to ensure that secondary flow does not exceed primary flow. When secondary flow exceeds primary flow, the secondary CHWST degrades (elevates), in turn causing lower secondary CHWRT. This in turn decreases the load on the chillers while causing the secondary flowrate to only increase further. Staging logic avoids this positive feedback scenario by staging up when the presence of secondary recirculation has been confirmed by secondary CHWST exceeding primary by 2°F.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Condensing Boiler Staging – Avoid Short Cycling
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For condensing boilers, staging up is delayed until the current stage output exceeds the minimum output of the next stage by 100% to avoid short cycling after stage up. The default threshold is set to ensure sufficient load to prevent cycling.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Staging is delayed until the current stage output exceeds the minimum output of the next stage by 100% to avoid boiler short cycling following stage up, which dramatically decreases plant efficiency. The default stage up threshold for the efficiency condition is set to ensure sufficient load to prevent boilers from short cycling and to create an adequate hysteresis to prevent unnecessary boiler staging, but the optimal threshold will depend in part on the boiler turndown.

evidence_quote: Staging is delayed until the current stage output exceeds the minimum output of the next stage by 100% to avoid boiler short cycling following stage up, which dramatically decreases plant efficiency. The default stage up threshold for the efficiency condition is set to ensure sufficient load to prevent boilers from short cycling and to create an adequate hysteresis to prevent unnecessary boiler staging, but the optimal threshold will depend in part on the boiler turndown.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Non-Condensing Boiler Staging – Simplified Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Non-condensing boilers do not benefit significantly from low turndown operation, so staging logic is simplified by running boilers to near full output (90% of design capacity) before staging up.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Non-condensing boilers do not benefit significantly from operating at low turndowns since the primary benefit of doing so is to maximize condensing, which is not permissible with non-condensing boiler heat exchangers. Logic is therefore simplified by running boilers to near full output prior to staging.

evidence_quote: Non-condensing boilers do not benefit significantly from operating at low turndowns since the primary benefit of doing so is to maximize condensing, which is not permissible with non-condensing boiler heat exchangers. Logic is therefore simplified by running boilers to near full output prior to staging.

structured_payload_candidate.title: HW Pump DP Setpoint Not Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: HW pump DP setpoint is not reset by valve position because valve position is already used to reset HWST, which saves more energy. Pump energy is converted to heat, so reductions in pump energy are offset by the boilers.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: HW pump DP setpoint is not reset by valve position because valve position is already used to reset HWST, which saves much more energy than DP reset. As noted above, pump energy is ultimately turned into heat so reductions in HW pump energy are made up by the boilers.

evidence_quote: HW pump DP setpoint is not reset by valve position because valve position is already used to reset HWST, which saves much more energy than DP reset. As noted above, pump energy is ultimately turned into heat so reductions in HW pump energy are made up by the boilers.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Condensation Protection – Variable Speed Primary Pumps Preferred
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Where variable speed primary pumps are provided, they should be used first for condensation protection because increasing primary pump speed to raise boiler entering temperature does not starve loads like limiting secondary pump speed does.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Where variable speed primary pumps are provided, they should be used first to provide condensation protection since increasing primary pump speed to increase boiler entering temperature does not starve loads like limiting secondary pump speed (or using thermostatic mixing valves for that matter) does.

evidence_quote: Where variable speed primary pumps are provided, they should be used first to provide condensation protection since increasing primary pump speed to increase boiler entering temperature does not starve loads like limiting secondary pump speed (or using thermostatic mixing valves for that matter) does.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Constant Speed Secondary Pumps Not Recommended
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Constant speed secondary pumps are generally not advisable for any boiler system. For non-condensing systems, VFDs are more cost-effective for condensation protection. For condensing systems, variable primary systems are simpler and provide equal or better performance.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Constant speed secondary pumps are generally not advisable on any boiler system. For non-condensing boiler systems, secondary pump VFDs are a more cost-effective means of providing condensation protection than 3-way thermostatic mixing valves and provide energy benefits as discussed in Section 5.21.5. For condensing boiler systems, constant speed secondary pumps could in theory be provided in conjunction with variable speed primary pumps to still allow for condensing operation at low loads, but a better option would be to simply provide a variable-primary system with either variable speed or constant speed pumps. The controls are simpler and as good or better energy performance will result.

evidence_quote: Constant speed secondary pumps are generally not advisable on any boiler system. For non-condensing boiler systems, secondary pump VFDs are a more cost-effective means of providing condensation protection than 3-way thermostatic mixing valves and provide energy benefits as discussed in Section 5.21.5. For condensing boiler systems, constant speed secondary pumps could in theory be provided in conjunction with variable speed primary pumps to still allow for condensing operation at low loads, but a better option would be to simply provide a variable-primary system with either variable speed or constant speed pumps. The controls are simpler and as good or better energy performance will result.
